message:
fix(cass): implement indentation-sensitive pattern comparison in mining

Fixes pattern matching in CASS session mining to properly handle
indentation differences, preventing false negatives when comparing
code patterns that are semantically identical but differently indented.

Changes to src/cass/mining.rs:

Indentation normalization:
- Strip leading whitespace before pattern comparison
- Preserve relative indentation within patterns
- Handle mixed tabs/spaces consistently
- Normalize line endings (CRLF -> LF)

Pattern comparison improvements:
- compare_patterns() now indentation-aware
- Configurable strictness levels:
  - Strict: Exact whitespace matching
  - Normal: Normalize leading indent (default)
  - Loose: Ignore all whitespace
- Better handling of empty lines in patterns

Impact on skill extraction:
- Before: "  if x:" != "if x:" (false negative)
- After: Both normalize to "if x:" (correct match)
- Improves pattern deduplication during mining
- Better extraction of reusable patterns

Changes to src/cass/transformation.rs:

Pattern transformation updates:
- Apply normalization during transformation
- Preserve original indentation in output
- Handle indent-sensitive languages (Python, YAML)
- Document indentation handling behavior

Test coverage:
- Added tests for various indentation scenarios
- Tab vs space handling verification
- Mixed indentation edge cases
- Empty line preservation tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
